title: cerastium decalvans subsp. durmitoreum
intro: Cerastium decalvans subsp. durmitoreum, also known as Durmitor's mouse ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is native to the Durmitor Mountains in Montenegro. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Cerastium decalvans subsp. durmitoreum include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
